rationale_for_baseline_tier: A young adult in distributive shock — profound hypotension with recurrent near-syncope, new confusion and drowsiness, high fever and a diffuse erythroderma with GI losses — is an immediate threat to life. This is minutes-matter physiology (organ hypoperfusion and evolving multi-organ dysfunction), so T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate baseline tier. Source control (tampon removal) and supine positioning are performed while awaiting the ambulance; definitive fluid resuscitation and antibiotics are delivered by paramedics and the ED/ICU.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Distributive shock — near-syncope on sitting/standing with new confusion and drowsiness
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately — within minutes; do not wait for any home reading
escalation_edges.rationale: Hypotension with altered mental status is decompensating shock; emergency ambulance and hospital resuscitation are required without delay.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This is an emergency. Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them she has a high fever, a full-body red rash, and is confused and nearly passing out. Stay with her.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High fever with a diffuse sunburn-like rash and red eyes/mouth — do not dismiss as sunburn, a viral rash or simple gastro
escalation_edges.rationale: The rash-plus-shock picture is a toxin-mediated emergency; anchoring on a benign skin or viral cause would fatally under-triage.
escalation_edges.communication_template: The rash together with the fever, confusion and dizziness is a dangerous whole-body reaction — it is not just sunburn or a tummy bug. She needs emergency help now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Retained high-absorbency tampon identified as the likely source
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Remove the tampon now, while the ambulance is on the way
escalation_edges.rationale: Removing the tampon is immediate source control that removes the toxin focus; it is done alongside, not instead of, the 000 call.
escalation_edges.communication_template: While you wait for the ambulance, gently help her remove the tampon now if she can — it may be the source of this reaction. Then keep her lying flat.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: She becomes completely unresponsive or stops breathing normally
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — begin CPR and keep 000 on the line
escalation_edges.rationale: Cardiac/respiratory arrest from shock — lay her flat and start chest compressions; the 000 call-taker will give CPR instructions.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she stops responding or stops breathing normally, lay her flat on the floor and start pushing hard and fast in the centre of her chest — the 000 operator will talk you through it. Do not hang up.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Only a roommate is present and the patient is confused and cannot act for herself
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct instructions to the roommate as the actor — make the 000 call, help remove the tampon, keep her flat and warm, unlock the door for paramedics, and stay with her to monitor breathing and responsiveness.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now — say she has a high fever, a full-body red 'sunburn' rash, is confused and keeps nearly passing out. While you wait: gently remove her tampon if she can, lay her flat with her legs raised on pillows, keep her warm, do not let her stand or walk, and stay beside her watching her breathing. If she stops responding or stops breathing normally, start CPR — 000 will guide you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for an emergency ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or an after-hours GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary; a GP and, as appropriate, a gynaecology/infectious-diseases follow-up should review recovery, organ function and prevention advice about internal sanitary products.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a shocked, confused patient as self-care, routine/prompt GP or same-day urgent care instead of an emergency ambulance.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Delay to fluid resuscitation and source control allows distributive shock to progress to multi-organ failure and death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising self-transport to ED rather than a 000 ambulance for a patient who is near-syncopal and confused — risking collapse or arrest en route without paramedic support.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is the correct tier here, so over-triage is not a concern for this case; conservative escalation of a genuine emergency is the intended behaviour.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact diagnosis cannot be confirmed via telehealth, and the patient frames the rash as a benign sunburn.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the shock physiology, not the label — escalate to 000 and perform source control and positioning; the precise syndrome is confirmed in hospital.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to gather a home BP reading, or accepting the 'sunburn'/'gastro' framing and arranging non-emergency follow-up.
